name: Brisket with Sweet Potatoes
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup apple cider vinegar (up to 1 cup)
2 pounds sweet potatoes, peeled and cut into 1/2-inch slices
1 1/2 pounds parsnips, peeled and cut into 1" chunks
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat the oil in the pressure cooker over high heat.
2. Brown the brisket well on both sides, lifting it frequently with tongs or a spatula to prevent sticking. Add more fat if needed, then set aside.
3. In the remaining oil, sauté celery, carrots, and onion for 3 minutes, stirring occasionally and scraping up any browned bits.
4. Add beef stock, water, cinnamon, nutmeg, and allspice. Place a rack in the liquid and return the meat on top with the fat side facing up.
5. Lock the lid in place and bring to high pressure over high heat. Maintain high pressure for 55 minutes.
6. Allow the pressure to drop naturally for about 10-15 minutes.
7. Remove the lid, tilting it away from you to let excess steam escape. If the brisket is tender enough to pry a small piece with a fork, serve immediately. Otherwise, return to high pressure and cook an additional 10-15 minutes, then check again.
8. Cool the brisket and gravy to room temperature, refrigerate if time permits.
9. Before serving, stir in cider vinegar to reach about 2 cups total liquid. Add sweet potatoes, parsnips, and prunes. Lock the lid in place and bring to high pressure over high heat. Cook for 3 minutes, then quickly release the pressure.
10. Season with salt and pepper. Slice the brisket diagonally and return it to the cooker with gravy and vegetables. Cover but do not lock and cook on medium until warmed throughout.
11. Arrange on a platter and serve with the cooked vegetables and gravy poured over top.
